component.name: Steps
component.description: Vertical numbered step list with status-aware indicators and a connector line between steps. Use 'progress' variant for actionable flows tracking completion, 'display' variant for static process documentation.

useCases: Process documentation showing a sequence of stages

commonPatterns.name: progress-flow
commonPatterns.description: Default variant — tracks user progress through a flow with check marks for completed steps and a primary border on the current step
commonPatterns.composition:
<Steps>
  <Step status="complete" number={1}>Account created</Step>
  <Step status="current" number={2}>Verify email</Step>
  <Step status="upcoming" number={3} last>Set up profile</Step>
</Steps>

commonPatterns.name: display-process
commonPatterns.description: Static documentation of a process — all dots use muted styling regardless of status
commonPatterns.composition:
<Steps variant="display">
  <Step status="complete" number={1}>Submit request</Step>
  <Step status="complete" number={2}>Review by team</Step>
  <Step status="complete" number={3} last>Receive response</Step>
</Steps>

commonPatterns.name: step-with-rich-content
commonPatterns.description: Steps render arbitrary children — combine titles with descriptions or other components
commonPatterns.composition:
Verify your email

Check your inbox for a confirmation link.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Steps for tabbed page navigation
antiPatterns.reason: Steps imply ordered progression; tabs imply parallel sibling views
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Tabs for switching between unrelated views

antiPatterns.scenario: Forgetting last={true} on the final step
antiPatterns.reason: The connector line continues past the last dot, leaving a dangling line
antiPatterns.alternative: Always set last on the final Step to hide the connector

antiPatterns.scenario: Using variant='display' for an actionable flow the user is moving through
antiPatterns.reason: Display mode hides the visual cue showing the user where they are
antiPatterns.alternative: Use the default 'progress' variant for any flow where status changes over time

semanticMeaning.progress: Default — tracks completion. Complete steps show a check on a primary background; current step has a primary border; upcoming steps are muted
semanticMeaning.display: Static documentation — all dots use muted styling, connector line is always border (never primary). Use when there is no notion of 'where the user is'

slots.indicator: Auto-rendered numbered dot or check icon based on status — not a separate slot
slots.content: children of Step renders to the right of the indicator; can be a single string or rich content (heading + paragraph + components)

interactions.static: Steps is non-interactive by default; render Buttons or links inside Step content for interactivity

responsive.mobile: Smaller indicator (size-5) and tighter gap (gap-2) for narrow viewports
responsive.desktop: Larger indicator (size-8) and wider gap (gap-6) for breathing room at md+

accessibility.role: Renders as <ol>; each Step renders as <li> with data-status
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Not interactive by default — interactive children (links/buttons) are individually tabbable
accessibility.screenReader: Reads as a numbered ordered list; status is conveyed via data-status (consider adding aria-current='step' on the current step in your usage if needed)
accessibility.focusManagement: No internal focus logic — managed by interactive children

rules: Always set last={true} on the final Step so the connector line does not extend past it
Use 'progress' (default) for actionable flows where status changes; use 'display' for static process documentation
Each Step needs a status: complete, current, or upcoming
Pass number for the dot label; check icon auto-replaces it for complete status in 'progress' variant
Children can be a simple label or rich content (heading + description + nested components)

aiHints.context: Use for ordered sequences where users move forward through stages. Default 'progress' variant tracks completion with check marks. Switch to 'display' variant for documentation of a process (no notion of current step). Always mark the last step with last={true}. Prefer Timeline for retrospective event lists where ordering is chronological rather than procedural.
